message:
history: add cross-session block counts

Close git_safety_guard-8sjj.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8kkm.4","title":"[Packs] Unit tests for schema, collisions, regex engine","description":"# Task: Unit tests for schema, collisions, regex engine

## Purpose
Validate custom pack authoring logic with deterministic unit tests.

## Requirements
- Schema version validation tests
- Pack ID collision behavior tests (override vs reject)
- Regex engine selection tests (linear vs backtracking)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover both valid and invalid packs
- Failure messages are actionable

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8kkm.5","title":"[Packs] E2E tests for custom pack validation + loading","description":"# Task: E2E tests for custom pack validation + loading

## Purpose
Ensure end-to-end custom pack flow works: validate → load → evaluate.

## Requirements
- Fixture YAML pack file(s)
- `dcg pack validate` success + failure cases
- Hook/CLI evaluation using custom pack enabled in config
- Verbose logging on failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests confirm custom pack influences evaluation
- Validation errors are clear and stable

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","title":"[PACK] backup.borg - BorgBackup Pack","description":"# BorgBackup Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Borg backup operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `borg delete` - Deletes archives or entire repo
- `borg prune` - Removes archives based on retention
- `borg compact` - After delete, reclaims space
- `borg recreate` - Can exclude data (data loss)
- `borg key export` then delete
- `borg break-lock` - Force breaks locks

## Safe Patterns
- `borg list`
- `borg info`
- `borg diff`
- `borg check`
- `borg create` (backup)
- `borg extract` (restore)
- `borg mount`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: backup.* (git_safety_guard-alc)
- Keywords: borg
- Note: prune with wrong retention is common mistake

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Archive deletion blocked
- [ ] Prune operations blocked
- [ ] Repository deletion blocked
- [ ] Create/extract allowed
- [ ] Tests cover common Borg operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T19:32:51.689713034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:41:59.506074785Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:41:59.506074785Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:47:08.701531034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:31.227367778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:31.311857467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:31.188671791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:31.149962920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:31.270813031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:07:31.352077572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T22:47:08.655715875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-8sjj","title":"[E10-T3] Implement cross-session tracking via history","description":"## Task: Implement cross-session tracking via history

### Parent Epic
Epic 10: Graduated Response System (git_safety_guard-yejh)

### What To Do
Query history database for cross-session occurrence counting.

### Implementation Details
1. History query:
   ```rust
   fn get_history_count(
       command: &str,
       window: Duration,
       db: &Database,
   ) -> Result<u32> {
       let cutoff = Utc::now() - window;
       
       sqlx::query_scalar!(
           r#"
           SELECT COUNT(*) as count
           FROM history
           WHERE command = ?
             AND timestamp > ?
             AND outcome = 'blocked'
           "#,
           command,
           cutoff,
       )
       .fetch_one(db)
       .await
   }
   ```

2. Alternative: hash-based grouping
   ```rust
   fn get_history_count_by_pattern(
       pattern_id: &str,  // Which pattern blocked it
       window: Duration,
       db: &Database,
   ) -> Result<u32> {
       // Count by pattern, not exact command
       // More useful for graduation
   }
   ```

3. Performance consideration:
   - Index on (command, timestamp) for fast queries
   - Consider caching recent counts
   - Window defaults to 24h (configurable)

### Files to Modify
- src/history/schema.rs (query function)
- src/history/mod.rs (exports)

### Dependencies
- E10-T1 (design specifies window and thresholds)
- Existing history database","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:51.573250452Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:53:33.204404917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8sjj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2at5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:15:53.001791449Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","title":"[DESIGN] Architectural Decisions for Pack Implementation","description":"# Architectural Decisions for Pack Implementation

## Overview
Before implementing new packs, several architectural decisions need to be documented and agreed upon. These decisions affect all packs and should be resolved BEFORE implementation begins.

## Decisions to Document

### 1. REST API Pattern Detection Strategy
**Problem**: Many tools use REST APIs via curl/httpie. How do we detect `curl -X DELETE api.example.com/resource`?

**Options**:
A. Keyword-based: Match `curl` + detect DELETE method + known API hostnames
B. AST-based: Parse curl arguments to extract method and URL
C. Hybrid: Quick keyword check, then AST parse for suspected matches

**Considerations**:
- Performance impact of AST parsing
- False positive rate for each approach
- Maintainability of hostname lists

### 2. Pack Overlap Resolution
**Problem**: cicd.github_actions and platform.github both cover `gh` commands

**Options**:
A. Strict separation: Each pack handles distinct commands
B. Layered: Platform pack is base, cicd pack extends
C. Merged: Single github pack with all patterns

**Considerations**:
- User configuration complexity
- Pattern duplication
- Maintenance burden

### 3. Command Alias Handling
**Problem**: `kubectl` vs `k`, `docker` vs `podman`, etc.

**Options**:
A. Explicit aliases: List all known aliases per pack
B. Keyword expansion: User configures their aliases
C. Pattern-based: Use regex to catch common alias patterns

**Considerations**:
- User customization needs
- Performance of expanded keyword lists

### 4. Performance Budget Per Pack
**Problem**: Each pack adds patterns. How do we prevent slowdown?

**Proposal**:
- Maximum patterns per pack: 50
- Maximum regex complexity score: TBD
- Benchmark requirement: <1ms per pack evaluation

### 5. Safe vs Destructive Threshold
**Problem**: Some commands are context-dependent (e.g., `rsync` is safe without --delete)

**Options**:
A. Flag-aware matching: Parse flags to determine safety
B. Conservative: Block ambiguous commands
C. Configurable: User sets their preference

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each decision documented with chosen option and rationale
- [ ] Performance benchmarks established
- [ ] Design doc created in docs/architecture/pack-design.md
- [ ] Team review completed

## Dependencies
- Should be resolved BEFORE implementing Tier 1 packs
- Informs testing strategy","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:09:53.183447098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T06:51:09.662426829Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T06:51:09.662426829Z","close_reason":"Documented pack architecture decisions in docs/architecture/pack-design.md; pending team review","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:35:21.510516334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ltou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:35:21.471784408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qdhh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:35:21.550636035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8uh0","title":"[E7-T1] Design security safeguards for interactive mode","description":"## Task: Design security safeguards for interactive mode

### Parent Epic
Epic 7: Interactive Learning Mode (git_safety_guard-2py1)

### What To Do
Design the security mechanism that prevents AI agents from automatically bypassing blocks.

### The Threat Model
- AI agents interact with dcg via stdin/stdout
- A malicious prompt injection could cause agent to send keystrokes
- If we add "press 'a' to allowlist", agent could send 'a'
- This would defeat dcg's entire purpose

### Safeguard Options Analysis

OPTION 1: Random Verification Code
```
To allowlist, type the code: 7x3k
>
```
- Pros: Simple, changes each time, can't be pre-scripted
- Cons: Could theoretically be OCR'd by advanced agent
- Strength: High (4 chars = 1.68M combinations)

OPTION 2: Timeout Requirement
```
Press 'a' within 3 seconds to allowlist...
[3] [2] [1] - Timeout, blocked
```
- Pros: Humans can react, scripts need timing
- Cons: Agents could potentially time responses
- Strength: Medium

OPTION 3: Full Command Typing
```
To allowlist, retype the command:
> rm -rf ./build
```
- Pros: Very hard to automate safely
- Cons: Tedious for users
- Strength: Very High

OPTION 4: CAPTCHA-like Challenge
```
What is 7 + 3? 
> 10
```
- Pros: Hard for simple scripts
- Cons: Trivial for LLM agents
- Strength: Low

RECOMMENDATION: Combine Options 1 + 2
- Random 4-character code
- 5-second timeout
- Code displayed, user must type it
- Timeout starts after code shown

### Configuration
```toml
[interactive]
enabled = true              # Master switch
verification = "code"       # code, command, none
timeout_seconds = 5
code_length = 4
```

### Files to Create
- docs/security-model.md (document design)

### Dependencies
- None - this is the design task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:11:16.473352987Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:38.049063863Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:38.049015301Z","close_reason":"Created docs/security-model.md with comprehensive threat model and dual-factor verification design for interactive mode security","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["security"]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","title":"Create comprehensive test suite for heredoc detection","description":"## Objective

Create comprehensive test coverage for heredoc detection and AST-based pattern matching, including unit tests, integration tests, and bypass attempt tests.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests

#### Heredoc Detection Tests

- Bash heredoc variants: `<<EOF`, `<<-EOF`, `<<<`, `<<'EOF'`, `<<"EOF"`
- Python inline scripts: `python3 -c ...`
- Node.js: `node -e ...`, `node <<EOF`
- Perl: `perl -e ...`, `perl <<'END'`
- Ruby: `ruby -e ...`, heredoc variants

#### Language Detection Tests

- Detection from command prefix (python3, node, ruby, perl, bash)
- Detection from shebang (`#!/usr/bin/env python3`)
- Detection from content heuristics (bounded)
- Fallback behavior for unknown languages

#### Pattern Matching Tests

- Each destructive pattern per language (high-signal)
- Each safe/negative pattern (things that look dangerous but aren't)
- Edge cases in quoting and escaping

### 2. Integration Tests

#### Pipeline Tests

- Full flow from JSON input to block/allow decision
- Heredoc commands correctly trigger deep analysis
- Non-heredoc commands still use fast path
- Performance within latency budget

#### Error Handling Tests

- Malformed heredoc syntax
- Unparseable content
- Unknown languages fall back gracefully
- Timeout handling (fail-open in hook mode)

### 3. Bypass Attempt Tests

Curate a set of realistic bypass attempts we want to cover, e.g.:

- variable indirection (`CMD=rm; $CMD -rf ...`)
- command substitution (`$(echo rm) -rf ...`)
- `bash -c` nesting

(These should be weighted toward high-signal, common bypasses; avoid building a giant speculative list that creates noisy tests.)

### 4. False Positive Tests

Ensure we DON'T block legitimate uses:

- documentation containing command examples
- grep/rg patterns searching for dangerous commands
- `bd create` descriptions that mention dangerous patterns

### 5. Performance Tests

- Tier 1 trigger latency
- extraction latency
- AST match latency (bounded)
- large heredoc handling (size limits)

## Test infrastructure sketch

```
tests/
  fixtures/
    heredocs/
    bypass_attempts/
    false_positives/
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test suite is structured so failures are easy to attribute (tier1/extract/lang/match).
- Includes a curated corpus that covers:
  - main heredoc forms
  - at least one example per supported language
  - a small set of realistic bypass attempts
  - a strong false-positive regression set
- Enforces policy behaviors:
  - hook fail-open on parse/timeout
  - severity mapping (deny vs warn)
- Provides clear logs/assert messages so CI failures are actionable.
","notes":"Added focused heredoc coverage: unit tests for <<~ indent-stripped extraction, quoted delimiter tracking, interpreter-prefix + shebang-based language detection; evaluator integration tests for heredoc deny/allow behavior + allowlist override; quality gates passed (cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + scripts/e2e_test.sh: 176/176).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFalcon","created_at":"2026-01-07T23:40:09.282575411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T12:06:42.366531098Z","closed_at":"2026-01-08T12:06:42.366531098Z","close_reason":"Completed (comprehensive heredoc unit+integration tests; all gates green)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T23:40:16.212012296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-unit","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T00:37:43.125748306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-unit","type":"supersedes","created_at":"2026-01-08T02:15:31.580734421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-08T02:49:53.774088173Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
